steps.title: Ship Faster — AI in Product Development
steps.body:
Product development is a cycle of ideas → validation → building → launching → learning. AI accelerates every phase.

Today you'll learn:
• Rapid prototyping with AI — from idea to working prototype in days, not weeks
• User research automation — gathering insights without manual interviews
• Feature prioritization — AI analyzes which features drive the most value
• Technical documentation — AI generates specs, API docs, and release notes

The best product teams don't use AI to replace their thinking. They use it to reduce the time between idea and validated learning.

Speed multipliers in product development:

• UI mockups: 1 hour → 5 minutes with AI design tools
• User story writing: 2 hours → 15 minutes with AI generation
• Competitor analysis: 1 day → 30 minutes with AI research
• PRD documents: 1 day → 1 hour with AI structuring
• QA test cases: 1 day → 2 hours with AI generation

The product teams shipping fastest aren't working harder. They're using AI to eliminate the non-differentiating work.

steps.question: A product manager wants to validate a new feature idea. What's the fastest AI-assisted validation approach?
options: Build the full feature, launch it, and see what happens
Ask AI to generate a survey, analyze competitor features, create mockups, and draft user testing scripts — then run lightweight tests in 2-3 days
Present the idea to the team and vote on it
Ask AI to decide whether to build the feature
feedback: Exactly. AI accelerates every validation step: survey generation, competitive analysis, mockup creation, and testing scripts. This lets you test an idea with real users in days instead of weeks — before committing to a full build.

Not quite. The fastest path is AI-assisted rapid validation: use AI to generate survey questions, build testable mockups from wireframes, research competitors, and draft test scripts. Validate with real users in days, then decide whether to build.

steps.title: PRD Generation Prompt
steps.prompt:
Turn a feature idea into a complete Product Requirements Document:

"I want to build [FEATURE DESCRIPTION] for [PRODUCT NAME].

Generate a PRD with:

1. Problem Statement — what problem does this solve, and for whom?
2. Success Metrics — how do we measure if this feature works?
3. User Stories — list 5-8 stories in 'As a [user], I want [goal], so that [reason]' format
4. Functional Requirements — what the feature must do (numbered, testable)
5. Non-Functional Requirements — performance, security, accessibility
6. Edge Cases — 3-5 potential failure modes
7. Out of Scope — explicitly what we're NOT building
8. Dependencies — what else needs to exist for this to work

For each section, flag anything that needs product manager judgment vs. what AI can draft."

The result: a 70% complete PRD in 10 minutes. The PM fills in the remaining 30% — the strategic decisions only they can make.

steps.title: AI-Assisted Product Sprint — The Buildly Story
steps.body:
Buildly is a 5-person startup with a big idea: AI-powered construction project management.

Week 1 (AI Accelerated): They use AI to:
• Research 12 competitor products → feature comparison matrix (2 hours → 15 min)
• Generate 30+ user stories from their target market interviews (3 days → 4 hours)
• Create clickable wireframes in Figma using AI design plugins (1 week → 1 day)
• Draft technical architecture document (2 days → 3 hours)

Week 2: User testing with 8 potential customers using AI-synthesized survey + interview guides
Week 3: AI generates test data, edge case scenarios, and draft documentation while developers code
Week 4: MVP ready — normally a 3-4 month process

Did AI write the code? No. Did AI replace the designers? No. But AI eliminated weeks of overhead that had nothing to do with their product's unique value.

steps.question: AI is most valuable in the early stages of product development (ideation, validation, prototyping) rather than in the late stages (production deployment, scaling).
feedback: Correct. AI's biggest product development impact is accelerating the Build-Measure-Learn loop at the fuzzy front end — faster ideation, validation, and prototyping. In production, AI adds value in testing, monitoring, and docs, but the core differentiator at scale is human judgment.
True. The early stages have the most ambiguity and the highest leverage for speed. AI eliminates weeks of overhead in research, mockups, documentation — getting you to validated learning faster.

steps.question: You need to prioritize 20 feature requests for your product's next quarter. You have data on estimated effort, user impact, revenue potential, and development dependencies.
feedback: Perfect for AI. Multiple structured data points with clear trade-offs is exactly what AI pattern analysis handles well. AI can weight each factor, surface conflicts, and rank features — while the PM applies strategic context AI can't know (company goals, partner commitments, market timing).

This is an ideal AI use case. Features have structured data (effort, impact, revenue) that AI can analyze and score. The PM provides the strategic context. Together, you get a prioritized roadmap that considers both data and judgment.

steps.title: AI in Each Product Phase
steps.body:
How AI accelerates every product development phase:

1. IDEATE — AI generates feature ideas from customer feedback, competitor analysis, and market trends

2. VALIDATE — AI drafts surveys, creates testable mockups, generates user testing scripts, analyzes results

3. SPEC — AI produces PRDs, user stories, acceptance criteria, and technical specs

4. BUILD — AI generates boilerplate code, test cases, documentation, and release notes

5. LAUNCH — AI creates launch materials, monitors launch metrics, flags anomalies

6. LEARN — AI analyzes usage data, surfaces patterns, generates retrospective summaries

AI doesn't replace a product manager. It gives them a team of assistants.

steps.title: Design an AI-Accelerated Product Sprint
steps.instruction:
Think of a product or feature you'd like to build. Design a 2-week AI-accelerated sprint:

Week 1 — Validation: What would you create in the first week using AI?
Week 2 — Build: What would AI help you produce?

For each phase, list:
• What AI generates
• What you (human) decide
• How long each step takes with vs. without AI
steps.placeholder:
Phase: Validation
AI generates: survey, competitor analysis, mockups
Human decides: target segment, core value proposition
Time saved: 5 days → 1 day

Phase: Build
AI generates: spec docs, test data, release notes
Human: architecture decisions, code quality, UX flows
Time saved: 3 days → 1 day

steps.title: User Research at AI Speed
steps.body:
Tomas needs to understand why user retention drops after the first week. Traditional approach: schedule 15 user interviews (2 weeks), manually transcribe and tag themes (1 week), write report (2 days).

AI-assisted approach:
1. Export all in-app behavior data for users who churned in week 1
2. AI analyzes behavioral patterns: which actions correlate with retention vs. churn (30 min)
3. AI drafts 5 user interview scripts targeting specific behavioral segments (15 min)
4. Record 5 interviews using AI transcription + theme extraction
5. AI generates retention hypothesis report with supporting evidence (1 hour)

Total: 2 days instead of 3 weeks. More data-driven, less manual labor.

Tomas doesn't talk to fewer users. He talks to the right users with better questions — and gets insights faster.

questions.prompt: What's the biggest bottleneck in your current product development process? Is it ideation, validation, building, or learning? How would AI accelerate that bottleneck?
questions.placeholder: The bottleneck and how AI accelerates it...

questions.prompt: If you could eliminate all documentation and specification writing from your product process, what would you do with that reclaimed time?
questions.placeholder: What you'd do with reclaimed time...

steps.body:
The product teams that will own their markets:

They don't ship faster because they have more developers.
They ship faster because they've eliminated every non-essential task between idea and learning.

AI is the elimination tool. Product judgment is the competitive advantage.

One without the other loses. Together, they win.
highlights: Product judgment is the competitive advantage

steps.title: Day 18 Complete!
steps.body:
You now understand how AI transforms product development — accelerating every phase from ideation through launch while keeping human judgment at the center. Rapid validation, AI-generated documentation, automated user research, and smarter prioritization.

Tomorrow: AI for Finance & Accounting — automated reconciliation, forecasting, and expense management.
